tags.name: MuMart (museo sommerso)
tags.description: Underwater art museum with contemporary sculptures anchored on sandy seabed at Golfo Aranci, 4-7m depth

tags.name: Cala Mariolu
tags.description: Protected Gulf of Orosei cove with cave-riddled limestone cliffs; walls extend below waterline with caverns and narrow tunnels. Visibility frequently exceeds 30m. Groupers common at cave entrances. Accessible only by boat or long coastal walk.

Largest marine cave in Mediterranean; full system approximately 4 km (Wikipedia) with ~10 entrances; individual dive routes cover 300-500m of passage. Depths 0-35m. Walls colonised by red coral and Leptopsammia pruvoti yellow cup corals; air chambers inside. Lobsters, conger eels, moray eels, groupers. Within Capo Caccia-Isola Piana MPA (est. 2002).

Dramatic vertical walls to 30m in Capo Caccia-Isola Piana MPA (est. 2002); caves, cracks, and overhangs carpeted in red coral and encrusting sponges. Moray eels and large groupers occupy deeper crevices. Barracuda patrol open water.

WWII German KriegsTransporter KT12, 834 tonnes, approximately 68 metres length (Italian dive operator sources cite 68m; some sources give 60m). Built OTO Livorno, commissioned May 1943. Torpedoed June 10 1943 by British submarine HMS Safari on maiden voyage with fuel drums and vehicles for North Africa. Bow separated and settled 700-800m from main hull; both sections rest at 27-34m off Orosei. Stern gun, propeller, rudder intact; cargo cranes on seabed. Groupers, moray eels, scorpionfish, slipper lobsters resident.
Tavolara-Punta Coda Cavallo MPA (est. 1997), 15,000 hectares, 76 km of coastline. Paleozoic granite formations; dusky groupers, moray eels, barracuda, damselfish; regulated zones with mooring buoys.

Three limestone pinnacles 300m east of Punta del Papa at Tavolara Island; Papa 1 shallowest at 15m, Papa 2 at 24m, base 45-50m. Damselfish clouds, large dusky groupers, barracuda, red and yellow gorgonian fans. Current always present, sometimes strong; Nitrox recommended. Two mooring buoys; anchoring prohibited.

Granite island in Capo Carbonara MPA; granite boulders create deep fissures to 40m. Madonna del Naufrago statue at 11m (placed 1979); granite monolith La Nave Romana to 40m; red gorgonian colony at Sandbank of Mezzo. Groupers, barracuda, sea bream, lobsters.

Gateway to Bue Marino sea caves on Gulf of Orosei; halocline from fresh spring water meeting salt sea inside cave chambers; depths to ~9m inside. Stalactites formed when caverns were above sea level. Rocky reef and posidonia meadows outside cave entrance. Boat access required; cave diving requires guided access.
Capo Carbonara MPA with granite boulders and pinnacles; large dusky groupers (Epinephelus marginatus), barracuda schools, corb, sparidae; visibility often exceeds 30m

Calm natural bay within Capo Caccia-Isola Piana MPA; posidonia oceanica meadows providing habitat for molluscs, crustaceans, and lobsters; rocky formations carry sea fans and encrusting corals. Dive access requires authorization via MPA-approved operators.
